title: Buy and Sell Stock - II | (DP -36)
content:
Problem Statement: 

Buy and Sell Stock - II 

We are given an array Arr[] of length n. It represents the price of a stock on ‘n’ days. The following guidelines need to be followed:

  1. We can buy and sell the stock any number of times.
  2. In order to sell the stock, we need to first buy it on the same or any previous day.
  3. We can’t buy a stock again after buying it once. In other words, we first buy a stock and then sell it. After selling we can buy and sell again. But we can’t sell before buying and can’t buy before selling any previously bought stock.

Examples 

Example 1:

Array={7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4}
N=6
MaxProfit=7 as ((5-1) + (6-3))
Explaination: Buy the stock on 1st day at price 1 and sell the stock on 2nd day at the price of 5. Then again buy stock at day 3 and sell the stock at 4
Practice:
Solve Problem

Disclaimer: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

Memoization approach Tabulation approach Space Optimization

Expand any one approach by clicking the given options in the bar. Clicking one approach on bar, closes all other expands. You can manually expand more than one approach at a time

Memoization Approach
Algorithm

Intuition

Every day, we have two options: either do nothing or make a buy/sell action to increase profit. Since we must check all possibilities to get the maximum profit, we use recursion.

Represent the problem
Think of the problem as decisions for each day. Keep track of whether we are allowed to buy or need to sell. We calculate the maximum profit possible from that day onward.

Try all choices

  • If we are allowed to buy:
    • Option 1: Skip the day and move to the next.
    • Option 2: Buy the stock (profit decreases now because we spend money) and then move to the next day.
  • If we are holding a stock and can sell:
    • Option 1: Skip the day and move to the next.
    • Option 2: Sell the stock (profit increases now because we earn money) and then move to the next day.

Return the maximum
For every day, choose the option that gives the highest profit.

Base Case

  • If we have reached the end of the trading days, return 0 as no more profit can be made.

Memoization

  1. Create a table to store already calculated results.
  2. Before solving for a day, check if it is already stored; if yes, use it directly.
  3. If not, calculate it using recursion and store it for future use.
Code
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
class StockProfit{
long getAns(long *Arr, int ind, int buy, int n, vector<vector<long>> &dp) {
    // Base case: When we reach the end of the array, there are no more decisions to make.
    if (ind == n) {
        return 0;
    }

    // If the result for this state has already been calculated, return it
    if (dp[ind][buy] != -1) {
        return dp[ind][buy];
    }

    long profit = 0;

    if (buy == 0) { // We can buy the stock
        profit = max(0 + getAns(Arr, ind + 1, 0, n, dp), -Arr[ind] + getAns(Arr, ind + 1, 1, n, dp));
    }

    if (buy == 1) { // We can sell the stock
        profit = max(0 + getAns(Arr, ind + 1, 1, n, dp), Arr[ind] + getAns(Arr, ind + 1, 0, n, dp));
    }

    // Store the calculated profit in the DP table and return it
    return dp[ind][buy] = profit;
}

long getMaximumProfit(long *Arr, int n) {
    // Create a DP table to memoize results
    vector<vector<long>> dp(n, vector<long>(2, -1));

    if (n == 0) {
        return 0;
    }

    long ans = getAns(Arr, 0, 0, n, dp);
    return ans;
}

int main() {
    int n = 6;
    long Arr[n] = {7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4};
    return 0;
}
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354
import java.util.Arrays;

public class StockProfit {
    // Recursive helper function to calculate maximum profit
    private long getAns(long[] arr, int ind, int buy, int n, long[][] dp) {
        // Base case: when we reach the end of the array, no more transactions are possible
        if (ind == n) {
            return 0;
        }

        // If this state is already computed, return it from the DP table
        if (dp[ind][buy] != -1) {
            return dp[ind][buy];
        }

        long profit;
        if (buy == 0) { 
            // Case when we are allowed to buy
            // Either skip buying (0 + getAns(...)) or buy (-arr[ind] + getAns(...))
            profit = Math.max(
                0 + getAns(arr, ind + 1, 0, n, dp),
                -arr[ind] + getAns(arr, ind + 1, 1, n, dp)
            );
        } else { 
            // Case when we are allowed to sell
            // Either skip selling or sell and add arr[ind] to profit
            profit = Math.max(
                0 + getAns(arr, ind + 1, 1, n, dp),
                arr[ind] + getAns(arr, ind + 1, 0, n, dp)
            );
        }

        // Store result in DP table and return
        dp[ind][buy] = profit;
        return profit;
    }

    public long getMaximumProfit(long[] arr, int n) {
        if (n == 0) return 0; // Edge case: empty array

        // Initialize DP table with -1 (meaning uncalculated)
        long[][] dp = new long[n][2];
        for (long[] row : dp) Arrays.fill(row, -1);

        return getAns(arr, 0, 0, n, dp);
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        StockProfit sp = new StockProfit();
        long[] prices = {7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4};
        System.out.println("Maximum Profit: " + sp.getMaximumProfit(prices, prices.length));
    }
}
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849505152535455
class StockProfit:
    def get_ans(self, arr, ind, buy, n, dp):
        """
        Recursive helper function to calculate maximum profit
        :param arr: List of stock prices
        :param ind: Current index (day)
        :param buy: 0 = can buy, 1 = can sell
        :param n: Total number of days
        :param dp: Memoization table
        :return: Maximum profit from this state onwards
        """
        # Base case: reached the end of the array
        if ind == n:
            return 0

        # Return already computed result
        if dp[ind][buy] != -1:
            return dp[ind][buy]

        if buy == 0:
            # Option 1: skip buying
            # Option 2: buy today (negative price because money spent)
            profit = max(
                0 + self.get_ans(arr, ind + 1, 0, n, dp),
                -arr[ind] + self.get_ans(arr, ind + 1, 1, n, dp)
            )
        else:
            # Option 1: skip selling
            # Option 2: sell today (add price to profit)
            profit = max(
                0 + self.get_ans(arr, ind + 1, 1, n, dp),
                arr[ind] + self.get_ans(arr, ind + 1, 0, n, dp)
            )

        # Store and return
        dp[ind][buy] = profit
        return profit

    def get_maximum_profit(self, arr):
        n = len(arr)
        if n == 0:
            return 0

        # Initialize DP table with -1
        dp = [[-1] * 2 for _ in range(n)]

        return self.get_ans(arr, 0, 0, n, dp)


# Driver code
if __name__ == "__main__":
    sp = StockProfit()
    prices = [7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4]
    print("Maximum Profit:", sp.get_maximum_profit(prices))
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253545556575859
class StockProfit {
    /**
     * Recursive helper function to calculate maximum profit
     * @param {number[]} arr - Stock prices array
     * @param {number} ind - Current index (day)
     * @param {number} buy - 0 = can buy, 1 = can sell
     * @param {number} n - Total number of days
     * @param {number[][]} dp - Memoization table
     * @returns {number} Maximum profit from this state onwards
     */
    getAns(arr, ind, buy, n, dp) {
        // Base case: reached the end of the array
        if (ind === n) {
            return 0;
        }

        // Return cached result
        if (dp[ind][buy] !== -1) {
            return dp[ind][buy];
        }

        let profit;
        if (buy === 0) {
            // Option 1: skip buying
            // Option 2: buy today (negative price)
            profit = Math.max(
                0 + this.getAns(arr, ind + 1, 0, n, dp),
                -arr[ind] + this.getAns(arr, ind + 1, 1, n, dp)
            );
        } else {
            // Option 1: skip selling
            // Option 2: sell today (add price)
            profit = Math.max(
                0 + this.getAns(arr, ind + 1, 1, n, dp),
                arr[ind] + this.getAns(arr, ind + 1, 0, n, dp)
            );
        }

        // Store and return
        dp[ind][buy] = profit;
        return profit;
    }

    getMaximumProfit(arr) {
        const n = arr.length;
        if (n === 0) return 0;

        // Initialize DP table with -1
        const dp = Array.from({ length: n }, () => [-1, -1]);

        return this.getAns(arr, 0, 0, n, dp);
    }
}

// Driver code
const sp = new StockProfit();
const prices = [7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4];
console.log("Maximum Profit:", sp.getMaximumProfit(prices));
Complexity Analysis

Time Complexity: O(N*2). There are N*2 states therefore at max ‘N*2’ new problems will be solved and we are running a for loop for ‘N’ times to calculate the total sum

Space Complexity: O(N*2) + O(N). We are using a recursion stack space(O(N)) and a 2D array ( O(N*2)).

Tabulation Approach
Algorithm

To change the memoization method into a tabulation method, we create a table (dp array) of the same size. We fill it step by step instead of using recursion.

  • First, we make a table with rows for each day and two columns for the two possible states (buy or sell). Initially, all values are set to zero.
  • We then set the base condition: for the last day (when no more trading is possible), both buying and selling give zero profit.
  • Next, we use two loops:
    • The outer loop moves from the second last day back to the first day.
    • The inner loop goes through both states (buying and selling).
  • For every day and state, we fill the table using the same choice logic as in the memoization method (choosing the maximum profit between doing nothing and making a transaction).
  • Finally, the answer is stored in the first cell, which gives the maximum profit starting from the first day when buying is allowed.
Code
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
class StockProfit {
long getMaximumProfit(long *Arr, int n) {
    // Create a DP table to memoize results
    vector<vector<long>> dp(n + 1, vector<long>(2, -1));

    // Base condition: When we have no stocks left, the profit is 0.
    dp[n][0] = dp[n][1] = 0;

    long profit;

    // Loop through the array in reverse order
    for (int ind = n - 1; ind >= 0; ind--) {
        for (int buy = 0; buy <= 1; buy++) {
            if (buy == 0) { // We can buy the stock
                profit = max(0 + dp[ind + 1][0], -Arr[ind] + dp[ind + 1][1]);
            }

            if (buy == 1) { // We can sell the stock
                profit = max(0 + dp[ind + 1][1], Arr[ind] + dp[ind + 1][0]);
            }

            dp[ind][buy] = profit;
        }
    }

    // The maximum profit is stored in dp[0][0] after all calculations
    return dp[0][0];
}

int main() {
    int n = 6;
    long Arr[n] = {7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4};

    // Call the getMaximumProfit function and print the result
    cout << "The maximum profit that can be generated is " << getMaximumProfit(Arr, n);

    return 0;
}
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051
import java.util.Arrays;

public class StockProfit {

    public long getMaximumProfit(long[] arr, int n) {
        // Edge case: If no prices available, return 0
        if (n == 0) return 0;

        // DP table where dp[ind][buy] stores max profit from index 'ind' onwards
        // buy = 0 → can buy, buy = 1 → can sell
        long[][] dp = new long[n + 1][2];

        // Base condition: When at day n (beyond last), no profit can be made
        dp[n][0] = dp[n][1] = 0;

        // Iterate from the end of the array towards the beginning
        for (int ind = n - 1; ind >= 0; ind--) {
            for (int buy = 0; buy <= 1; buy++) {
                long profit;
                if (buy == 0) {
                    // We can buy → two options:
                    // 1. Skip buying → profit remains dp[ind+1][0]
                    // 2. Buy → pay the price (-arr[ind]) and move to selling state
                    profit = Math.max(
                        0 + dp[ind + 1][0],
                        -arr[ind] + dp[ind + 1][1]
                    );
                } else {
                    // We can sell → two options:
                    // 1. Skip selling → profit remains dp[ind+1][1]
                    // 2. Sell → gain price and move to buying state
                    profit = Math.max(
                        0 + dp[ind + 1][1],
                        arr[ind] + dp[ind + 1][0]
                    );
                }
                dp[ind][buy] = profit;
            }
        }

        // Final result is starting from index 0 with buying allowed
        return dp[0][0];
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        StockProfit sp = new StockProfit();
        long[] prices = {7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4};
        System.out.println("The maximum profit that can be generated is " + sp.getMaximumProfit(prices, prices.length));
    }
}
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344
class StockProfit:
    def get_maximum_profit(self, arr):
        """
        Calculates maximum stock trading profit using bottom-up DP
        :param arr: List of stock prices
        :return: Maximum achievable profit
        """
        n = len(arr)
        if n == 0:
            return 0

        # DP table: dp[ind][buy] → max profit from index 'ind'
        dp = [[0] * 2 for _ in range(n + 1)]

        # Base condition: dp[n][0] = dp[n][1] = 0 (no profit beyond last day)

        # Fill table from end to start
        for ind in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            for buy in range(2):
                if buy == 0:
                    # Option 1: skip buying
                    # Option 2: buy → -arr[ind] + dp[ind+1][1]
                    profit = max(
                        0 + dp[ind + 1][0],
                        -arr[ind] + dp[ind + 1][1]
                    )
                else:
                    # Option 1: skip selling
                    # Option 2: sell → arr[ind] + dp[ind+1][0]
                    profit = max(
                        0 + dp[ind + 1][1],
                        arr[ind] + dp[ind + 1][0]
                    )
                dp[ind][buy] = profit

        return dp[0][0]


# Driver code
if __name__ == "__main__":
    sp = StockProfit()
    prices = [7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4]
    print("The maximum profit that can be generated is", sp.get_maximum_profit(prices))
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647
class StockProfit {
    /**
     * Calculates maximum stock trading profit using bottom-up DP
     * @param {number[]} arr - Stock prices array
     * @returns {number} Maximum achievable profit
     */
    getMaximumProfit(arr) {
        const n = arr.length;
        if (n === 0) return 0;

        // DP table: dp[ind][buy] → max profit from index 'ind'
        const dp = Array.from({ length: n + 1 }, () => [0, 0]);

        // Base condition: dp[n][0] = dp[n][1] = 0 (no profit beyond last day)

        // Fill table from end to start
        for (let ind = n - 1; ind >= 0; ind--) {
            for (let buy = 0; buy <= 1; buy++) {
                let profit;
                if (buy === 0) {
                    // Option 1: skip buying
                    // Option 2: buy → -arr[ind] + dp[ind+1][1]
                    profit = Math.max(
                        0 + dp[ind + 1][0],
                        -arr[ind] + dp[ind + 1][1]
                    );
                } else {
                    // Option 1: skip selling
                    // Option 2: sell → arr[ind] + dp[ind+1][0]
                    profit = Math.max(
                        0 + dp[ind + 1][1],
                        arr[ind] + dp[ind + 1][0]
                    );
                }
                dp[ind][buy] = profit;
            }
        }

        return dp[0][0];
    }
}

// Driver code
const sp = new StockProfit();
const prices = [7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4];
console.log("The maximum profit that can be generated is", sp.getMaximumProfit(prices));
Complexity Analysis

Time Complexity: O(N*2). Reason: There are two nested loops that account for O(N*2) complexity.

Space Complexity: O(N*2). We are using an external array of size ‘N*2’. Stack Space is eliminated.

Space Optimization Approach
Algorithm

If we look closely at the pattern,

Each value depends only on the next day’s values.

This means we don’t need to keep the entire table. We can store only two rows (current and next), making the solution more memory-efficient.

  • We start by setting the "next day" row with the base condition (profit is 0 when no more days are left).
  • Then we run loops to fill the current day’s values.
  • Instead of referring to the entire table, we replace future references with the "next day" row and current references with the "current day" row.
  • After finishing calculations for the current day, we copy it into the "next day" row for the next iteration.
  • Finally, the first cell of the current row gives the maximum profit.

Code
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;
class StockProfit {
long getMaximumProfit(long *Arr, int n) {
    // Create two arrays to store the profits ahead of the current position (0 for not holding, 1 for holding)
    vector<long> ahead(2, 0);
    vector<long> cur(2, 0);

    // Base condition: When there are no stocks left, the profit is 0.
    ahead[0] = ahead[1] = 0;

    long profit;

    // Loop through the array in reverse order
    for (int ind = n - 1; ind >= 0; ind--) {
        for (int buy = 0; buy <= 1; buy++) {
            if (buy == 0) { // We can buy the stock
                profit = max(0 + ahead[0], -Arr[ind] + ahead[1]);
            }

            if (buy == 1) { // We can sell the stock
                profit = max(0 + ahead[1], Arr[ind] + ahead[0]);
            }
            cur[buy] = profit;
        }

        ahead = cur; // Update the "ahead" array with the current values
    }

    // The maximum profit is stored in cur[0] after all calculations
    return cur[0];
}

int main() {
    int n = 6;
    long Arr[n] = {7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4};

    // Call the getMaximumProfit function and print the result
    cout << "The maximum profit that can be generated is " << getMaximumProfit(Arr, n);

    return 0;
}
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445
public class StockProfit {

    public long getMaximumProfit(long[] arr, int n) {
        if (n == 0) return 0;

        // Arrays to store profit for future days
        // ahead → profit for the next day
        // cur → profit for the current day being processed
        long[] ahead = new long[2]; // initialized to 0
        long[] cur = new long[2];   // initialized to 0

        // Base condition: no profit if no transactions can be made
        ahead[0] = ahead[1] = 0;

        // Iterate from the last day to the first day
        for (int ind = n - 1; ind >= 0; ind--) {
            for (int buy = 0; buy <= 1; buy++) {
                long profit;
                if (buy == 0) {
                    // Option 1: skip buying
                    // Option 2: buy today → subtract price and move to selling state
                    profit = Math.max(0 + ahead[0], -arr[ind] + ahead[1]);
                } else {
                    // Option 1: skip selling
                    // Option 2: sell today → add price and move to buying state
                    profit = Math.max(0 + ahead[1], arr[ind] + ahead[0]);
                }
                cur[buy] = profit;
            }
            // Update ahead for the next iteration
            ahead = cur.clone();
        }

        // Maximum profit is when we start at day 0 with buying allowed
        return cur[0];
    }

    public static void main(String[] args) {
        StockProfit sp = new StockProfit();
        long[] prices = {7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4};
        System.out.println("The maximum profit that can be generated is " +
                sp.getMaximumProfit(prices, prices.length));
    }
}
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041
class StockProfit:
    def get_maximum_profit(self, arr):
        """
        Space-optimized bottom-up DP for maximum stock trading profit.
        :param arr: List of stock prices
        :return: Maximum profit achievable
        """
        n = len(arr)
        if n == 0:
            return 0

        # ahead → future profit (next day)
        # cur → current day's profit
        ahead = [0, 0]
        cur = [0, 0]

        # Iterate from last day to first
        for ind in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            for buy in range(2):
                if buy == 0:
                    # Option 1: skip buying
                    # Option 2: buy today
                    profit = max(0 + ahead[0], -arr[ind] + ahead[1])
                else:
                    # Option 1: skip selling
                    # Option 2: sell today
                    profit = max(0 + ahead[1], arr[ind] + ahead[0])
                cur[buy] = profit

            # Move current results into ahead for the next iteration
            ahead = cur[:]

        return cur[0]


# Driver code
if __name__ == "__main__":
    sp = StockProfit()
    prices = [7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4]
    print("The maximum profit that can be generated is", sp.get_maximum_profit(prices))
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243
class StockProfit {
    /**
     * Space-optimized DP for maximum stock trading profit
     * @param {number[]} arr - Stock prices array
     * @returns {number} Maximum achievable profit
     */
    getMaximumProfit(arr) {
        const n = arr.length;
        if (n === 0) return 0;

        // ahead → profit for the next day
        // cur → profit for the current day being processed
        let ahead = [0, 0];
        let cur = [0, 0];

        // Iterate from last day to first
        for (let ind = n - 1; ind >= 0; ind--) {
            for (let buy = 0; buy <= 1; buy++) {
                let profit;
                if (buy === 0) {
                    // Option 1: skip buying
                    // Option 2: buy today
                    profit = Math.max(0 + ahead[0], -arr[ind] + ahead[1]);
                } else {
                    // Option 1: skip selling
                    // Option 2: sell today
                    profit = Math.max(0 + ahead[1], arr[ind] + ahead[0]);
                }
                cur[buy] = profit;
            }
            // Update ahead for next iteration
            ahead = [...cur];
        }

        return cur[0];
    }
}

// Driver code
const sp = new StockProfit();
const prices = [7, 1, 5, 3, 6, 4];
console.log("The maximum profit that can be generated is", sp.getMaximumProfit(prices));
Complexity Analysis

Time Complexity: O(N*2). Reason: There are two nested loops that account for O(N*2) complexity

Space Complexity: O(1). Reason: We are using an external array of size ‘2’.
